name: Reibekuchen (German Potatoe Pancake)
recipeIngredient: 1.5 kg potatoes (a variety that holds its shape when cooked, not suitable for mashed potatoes)
A small amount of flour (Buchweizen - wheat flour or oat flakes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate the potatoes through a sieve to allow excess water to drain.
2. Squeeze out as much liquid from the grated potatoes as possible.
3. Grate the onion and mix it with the potatoes, eggs, salt, and flour until well combined. Add more flour or oat flakes if needed to achieve a non-sticky dough.
4. Heat oil in a large pan.
5. Scoop small amounts of the batter into the hot oil and flatten with a spoon to form thin cakes.
6. Cook on both sides until golden brown and crispy around the edges (turn occasionally).
7. Serve hot on slices of dark bread with butter, and top with remoulade or apple syrup.
Note: Do not turn off the heat aggressively; allow the pan to cool naturally before cleaning.
